text.title: Strategy Analysis — Business Need — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Strategy Analysis — Business Need must enforce.

rule.statement: The business-need spec names a single accountable problem owner (sponsor).
rule.rationale: Problems without owners drift; the owner is the human who can answer 'is this still the problem?'.

rule.statement: Every problem claim cites at least one primary-source evidence URL with retrieved_at.
rule.rationale: Anecdote-based problem statements lead to solutioning the wrong problem.

rule.statement: The spec names exactly one quantified success metric with a definition + measurement source.
rule.rationale: Multi-metric specs invite later dilution; one metric forces clarity of intent.

rule.statement: The spec documents the do-nothing baseline (what happens if the initiative is not funded).
rule.rationale: Without the do-nothing comparator, ROI cannot be computed and funding cases overstate impact.

rule.statement: The spec MUST NOT name solutions, vendors, or implementation approaches.
rule.rationale: Solution language pre-empts options analysis and locks the initiative to one path before alternatives are explored.
